_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of target, including related current events, communication profile, actors, and history (language, culture) and/or frame of reference.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of NLP task taxonomy, including classification, named entity recognition, relation extraction, summarization, translation, and question answering, and the model architectures appropriate for each.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.477), DQ-K-008 (DQ, cosine: 0.453).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.7/10 (raw 0.272). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [actors, communication, culture, current, events, frame] vs WIDAI-only terms [answering, appropriate, architectures, classification, entity, extraction] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.851 (P=0.854, R=0.848). Balanced P/R — the 18-word DCWF element and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.6%). "Knowledge of target, including related current events, communication profile, ac" and "Knowledge of NLP task taxonomy, including classification, named entity recogniti" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.478 > STS 0.272 (gap +0.206). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [actors, communication, culture, current, events, frame]. WIDAI-only: [answering, appropriate, architectures, classification, entity, extraction].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.206 with low STS 0.272. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
